[OpenSpec Tutorial: Master Spec-Driven Development (SDD) [2026] | QubitTool](https://qubittool.com/blog/openspec-sdd-tutorial)\n4. [Spec-Driven Development with Claude Code | Build This Now](https://www.buildthisnow.com/blog/guide/mechanics/spec-driven-development)\n5. [Specification-Driven Development: How to Stop Vibe Coding and Actually Ship Production-Ready AI-Generated Code - Pockit Blog](https://pockit.tools/blog/specification-driven-development-ai-coding-agents-complete-guide/)\n\n# Spec-Driven Development (SDD): Four-Phase AI Coding Workflow\n\nSpec-Driven Development is a structured methodology for AI-assisted coding that prioritizes writing detailed specifications before implementation, replacing ad-hoc \"vibe coding\" with a disciplined, repeatable process.[1]\n\n## The Four-Phase Workflow\n\nSDD follows a consistent structure:[1][2]\n\n1. **Specify** — Write functional requirements from the user's perspective, including user stories, acceptance criteria, and edge cases. This is technology-agnostic and describes what the system does, not how it's built.[2]\n\n2. **Plan** — Define technical constraints, stack decisions, architectural patterns, and service boundaries. The AI produces an implementation plan grounded in the spec before any code is written.[1][2]\n\n3. **Break Down (Tasks)** — Convert the plan into concrete, testable tasks with clear inputs, outputs, and validation criteria. Dependencies and parallel work are explicitly flagged.[1][2]\n\n4. **Implement** — The AI agent works through the task list using the spec and plan as context for every decision. When divergence occurs, you update the spec rather than the code.[1]\n\n## Core Philosophy\n\nThe spec becomes the source of truth; code is treated as a disposable artifact that can be regenerated as needed.[1] This inverts traditional development where specs become outdated immediately. Unlike Waterfall, SDD compresses feedback loops to minutes rather than months — you can generate thousands of lines, discover issues, update the spec, and regenerate in an afternoon.[1]\n\n## Implementation Levels\n\nTeams adopt SDD at three commitment levels: spec-first (specs guide but code remains primary), spec-anchored (specs persist as governing contracts), and spec-as-source (specs are the primary artifact, code is a build artifact).[1]",

[Verified Spec-Driven Development Unifies TDD and VDD in AI-Native Engineering Pipeline | Trending Stories | HyperAI](https://hyper.ai/en/stories/f81e202b76dec8f9d007c4d90808f503)\n5. [Verified Spec-Driven Development](https://gist.github.com/dollspace-gay/d8d3bc3ecf4188df049d7a4726bb2a00?permalink_comment_id=6104313)\n\n# Verified Spec-Driven Development (VSDD) - 2026 Overview\n\n## What is VSDD?\n\n**Verified Spec-Driven Development (VSDD)** is an AI-native software engineering methodology that fuses three established disciplines into a sequential pipeline:[2][5]\n\n- **Spec-Driven Development (SDD)** — Define behavioral contracts before implementation\n- **Test-Driven Development (TDD)** — Write failing tests before code\n- **Verification-Driven Development (VDD)** — Subject code to adversarial refinement\n\nThese are not competing philosophies but sequential gates where specs define *what*, tests enforce *how*, and adversarial review ensures *nothing was missed*.[5]\n\n## The Core Problem It Solves\n\nVSDD addresses a critical gap in AI-assisted development: code that \"looks correct\" but contains hidden defects—what the methodology calls \"AI slop.\"[1] Standard LLM interactions are cooperative by design, creating blind spots when the same AI reviews its own work.[2]\n\n## Four Roles in the Pipeline\n\n1. **The Architect** (human) — Strategic vision, domain expertise, final authority[1]\n2. **The Builder** (Claude or similar AI) — Writes specs, tests, and code under strict TDD constraints[1]\n3. **The Tracker** (Chainlink) — Hierarchical issue decomposition linking specs to tests to code[1]\n4. **The Adversary** (fresh-context AI instance) — Hyper-critical reviewer with zero tolerance, runs on a different model family with no conversational history[1][2]\n\n## Six-Phase Pipeline\n\nThe methodology enforces six sequential phases: Spec Crystallization → Test-First Implementation → Adversarial Review → Feedback Integration → Formal Hardening → Convergence.[1]\n\n## Operating Modes\n\nVSDD offers two approaches:[1]\n- **Strict mode** — Full ceremony for high-assurance work with multiple adversary passes\n- **Lean mode** — All six phases remain but with lighter approvals for faster product iteration\n\n## Practical Adoption\n\nFor teams moving fast, the core insight can be simplified: write a 5-10 sentence behavioral spec before coding, then paste results into a fresh AI session for harsh critique—capturing 80% of VSDD value with 20% of the effort.[3]",

文档已生成到 `docbackup/AI辅助开发方法论对比分析-RFD-vs-SDD-VSDD-Superpowers.md`。

**核心结论总结**：

| 你的方法（RFD）的优势 | 主要短板 |
|---|---|
| AI 主动提问挖掘需求（业界独有） | 缺乏强制质量门控 |
| 迭代式结对编程体验 | 评审是"自己审自己" |
| 强制验收测试用例 | 无工作隔离机制 |
| 自动提交流水线 | 开发中无任务审批 |

**三个建议优先引入的改进**：

1. **对抗性评审** — 用**新会话**（无历史上下文）做 RFC 评审，解决合作偏差
2. **强制测试门控** — 所有测试通过前不允许提交
3. **git worktree 隔离** — 探索性开发在隔离分支中进行

这三个改进的成本都很低，但对质量保障的效果提升显著——本质上就是把你方法论中 AI 主动性的优势，往"下游质量保障"方向延伸了一步。

_**User**_

Actually, I'm reconsidering the terminology around TDD versus enhanced TDD — the key distinction is that in traditional test-driven development, tests define behavior before implementation, but in this workflow, the RFC serves as the primary specification that informs the test cases, so the tests aren't driving the design in the conventional sense.

Now I'm mapping out the full architecture of this system. The framework has three distinct phases: RFC generation captures the thinking process through an iterative, questioning approach that structures fragmented ideas into a coherent document. Looking at how this differs from traditional approaches, the key distinction is that human cognition isn't linear—it emerges in flashes and pieces—whereas documentation typically demands sequential presentation. The framework acknowledges this gap and uses AI's iterative questioning to bridge it, translating scattered thoughts into structured RFCs. In contrast, SDD treats code as disposable and code generation as the implementation mechanism, whereas this approach treats both code and documentation as equally valid artifacts with the RFC serving as the authoritative reference. The quality assurance also differs in emphasis and approach.

想的闪现性

文档已更新到第三版，新增了所有补充内容。以下是关键更新：

---

**新增内容：**

**1. RFC 生成的三大输入依赖**（第 1.1.2 节）

```
RFC 并非凭空产生，生成过程严格依赖三个条件：
① CORE-DESIGN.md + ACCEPTANCE.md（架构约束 + 验收标准）—— 技术边界
② 用户原始零星想法（闪现的碎片）—— 原材料
③ AI 初稿 + 迭代反馈循环 —— 催化剂
```

**2. 人类双重角色的显式定义**

```
人在 RFC 审核中同时承担：
① 需求规格的理解者 —— 业务意图判断（AI 无法代替）
② 架构实现的审核者 —— 技术可行性把关（需要架构知识）
→ 因此 RFC 审核者的要求是比较高的
```

**3. 软件本体的多视图愿景**（第 1.1.3 节 + 第 3.5 节）

```
软件 = 多视图本体，语义等价：
代码视图 ←→ 功能特性视图 ←→ 架构设计视图 ←→ 需求维护视图
开放问题：运行时代码包（JAR/WAR）如何定位？
```

---

**命名推荐：Epiphany-Driven Development（EDD）—— 顿悟驱动开发**

| 候选名 | 理由 | 风险 |
|--------|------|------|
| **EDD（推荐）** | `Epiphany`（顿悟）精准捕捉人类认知的闪现特性；AI 捕捉和结构化顿悟 | 非英语母语者不够直观 |
| FDD | `Flash` 简洁直观 | 与 Feature-Driven Dev 缩写冲突 |
| ODD | `Ontology` 对应多视图本体愿景 | 过于学术，易被误解 |
| CDD | `Crystallize` 隐喻优雅 | 较抽象 |
| IDD | `Insight` 易懂 | 含义宽泛 |

**核心推荐 EDD 的原因**：`Epiphany`（顿悟）精确捕捉了方法论的核心——设计想法不是线性推导的，而是从模糊碎片中涌现的顿悟，AI 的角色是捕捉和结构化，而非代替人类产生。

The user has made several important points:

1. **Ontology misconception**: The current methodology doesn't fully capture the multi-faceted nature of ontology - the same object can be seen differently from different perspectives. For example, a "bag" could be a "book bag" to one person, a "logistics item" to another, and a "product" to a third. The name/label changes with the perspective, but the underlying entity remains the same. This is a key insight that existing ontology research misses.

2. **Construction perspective**: Our understanding of reality is a process of gradual abstraction and layering. The construction approach itself is iterative. This aspect is not well represented in current methodologies including RFD/EDD.
   
3. **The document needs more philosophical depth**: I should expand the philosophical foundation section to better ground the methodology in established philosophical traditions rather than leaving it abstract.

So I need to restructure the document to include a dedicated chapter on the philosophical foundations—drawing from embodied cognition, situated knowledge, and the epistemology of design—to make the theoretical basis more explicit and substantive.

[Perspectivism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/perspectivism)\n\n# Perspectival Ontology: Heidegger, Gadamer, and Perspectivism\n\n## Core Philosophical Concept\n\nPerspectivism is an epistemological principle asserting that \"perception of and knowledge of something are always bound to the interpretive perspectives of those observing it.\"[5] Rather than viewing all perspectives as equally valid, perspectivism holds that \"no one has access to an absolute view of the world cut off from perspective\" and that \"all such viewing occurs from some point of view which in turn affects how things are perceived.\"[5]\n\n## Heidegger's Phenomenological Perspectivism\n\nHeidegger's approach integrates phenomenology, hermeneutics, and ontology.[3] He establishes a triple identification: \"phenomenology equals ontology equals hermeneutics.\"[3] Crucially, \"phenomenology simply is ontology,\" where both disciplines have the same object—being itself.[3] This framework results in what has been called \"phenomenological perspectivism,\" which emphasizes that \"hermeneutic\" interpretation is fundamental to ontological understanding.[1][3]\n\n## The Central Question\n\nThe philosophical problem underlying perspectivism addresses: \"To what extent do our experiences permit us to perceive the true nature of reality, and to what extent do they prevent us from doing so by merely reflecting features of our modes of perception which we are prone to mistake for features of reality?\"[2] Or more simply: \"What belongs to the essence of things and what merely to our perspective?\"[2]\n\n## Historical Context\n\nFriedrich Nietzsche developed perspectivism's first major philosophical statement in the 19th century, asking whether \"the perspectival is of the essence of the matter\" or merely \"a form of viewing.\"[2]\n\n**Note:** The search results do not contain substantive information about Gadamer's specific contributions to perspectival ontology.",

Thesis\n\nEmbodied cognition is a research program rejecting traditional computational cognitive science's view of the mind as symbol manipulation divorced from the body.[1] Instead, it emphasizes that \"the body or the body's interactions with the environment constitute or contribute to cognition.\"[1] Central to this framework is the idea that thought is physical, carried out by neural circuitry connected to bodily experience.[2][4]\n\n## Lakoff and Johnson's Contribution\n\nGeorge Lakoff and Mark Johnson developed **experiential realism** (also called experientialism or embodied realism) as a philosophical position grounded in cognitive linguistics.[5] They argue that abstract ideas—not just concrete ones—are fundamentally embodied.[2][4] Rather than viewing concepts as inherently meaningless abstract symbols that somehow represent external reality, Lakoff and Johnson show that meaning arises from how neural circuits connect to embodied experience.[2][4]\n\nTheir approach challenges the Chomskyan linguistic tradition and earlier generative semantics work, demonstrating through concrete examples that meaning cannot be extracted from disembodied symbol manipulation.[2][4]\n\n## The Neural Theory of Thought and Language (NTTL)\n\nLakoff's framework explains embodied cognition results through NTTL, which operates primarily via the **neural theory of conceptual metaphor.**[2][4] This theory accounts for how abstract thought is grounded in sensorimotor experience through metaphorical mapping.\n\n## Epistemological Implications\n\nThe embodied cognition approach reshapes how we understand minds, concepts, and representation itself,[1] challenging Cartesian dualism and traditional correspondence theories of truth by grounding knowledge in lived bodily experience rather than abstract mental representations.",

[](https://george-lakoff.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/04/metamorphical-issues-a-figure-of-thought-lakoff-1987.pdf)\n\n# Conceptual Metaphor Theory: Lakoff and Johnson's Framework\n\nConceptual metaphor theory, developed by George Lakoff and Mark Johnson in the late 1970s, fundamentally challenges traditional views of metaphor as merely poetic or rhetorical language.[1][4][5]\n\n## Core Principles\n\n**The essence of metaphor is understanding and experiencing one kind of thing in terms of another.**[2] Rather than being a device of extraordinary language, metaphor is pervasive in everyday life—it shapes thought, action, and perception.[2] The theory demonstrates that our ordinary conceptual system is fundamentally metaphorical in nature, governing how we think, perceive, and interact with the world.[2]\n\n## Key Metaphor Types\n\nLakoff and Johnson identified several major categories:\n\n**Ontological Metaphors**: Allow us to understand experiences as discrete entities or substances, enabling reference, categorization, quantification, and reasoning. For instance, IDEAS ARE OBJECTS, LANGUAGE IS A CONTAINER FOR IDEA-OBJECTS, and COMMUNICATION IS SENDING IDEA-OBJECTS IN LANGUAGE-CONTAINERS.[4]\n\n## Connection to Values and Culture\n\nCultural values cohere with metaphorical structures. [](https://gwern.net/doc/philosophy/epistemology/1968-popper.pdf)\n\n# Karl Popper's Three Worlds and Information Architecture\n\n## The Three Worlds Framework\n\nKarl Popper developed his three worlds theory in the late 1960s to defend the notion of objective knowledge.[1] The framework consists of:\n\n- **World 1**: The material realm of physical objects, events, and processes[1]\n- **World 2**: The mental realm of states of consciousness, beliefs, and psychological phenomena[1]\n- **World 3**: The cultural realm of objective contents of thought, including scientific theories, artistic works, problems, critical arguments, and the contents of books and libraries[1][5]\n\n## Objective Knowledge in World 3\n\nPopper's key innovation was distinguishing between subjective knowledge (beliefs held in World 2) and objective knowledge existing in World 3.[1] For Popper, \"objective\" did not mean \"true\" or \"certain\"—rather, objective knowledge consists of fallible, sharable assertions that survive criticism and empirical testing.[4] The methodological rules and logical content of science belong to World 3.[1]\n\n## Applications to Information Science and Systems\n\nInformation science's task is to \"collect and order World 3 artefacts in such a way that they can be accessed by an individual's knowledge structure,\" involving study of World 2-World 3 interactions.[2] Popper's three worlds ontology provides foundations for information systems to integrate different information professions and address knowledge domains with potentially incompatible conceptual structures.[3]\n\nThe results provided do not directly address software architecture, semantic web, or ontology in technical detail.",

文档已更新到第四版。以下是主要新增内容：

---

**一、新增第 3.6 节「现有本体方法论的认知局限」**

这一节是针对你指出的"现有本体论忽视了两个关键维度"而写的：

**维度一：视角依赖性（Perspectival Nature）**

> 同一个"书包"，在学生眼中是收纳工具，在工厂工人眼中是产品，在物流工人眼中是物流单元，在零售商眼中是商品。现有语义网本体论的 `owl:Thing` 继承了"概念是客观存在的"这一哲学立场，忽视了视角依赖性。

EDD 的修正：承认不同视角的**不可通约性**，通过多视图等价映射而非单一全局本体来解决。

**维度二：命名多样性与语义等价（Milingual Naming）**

> 同一个实体，在不同人口中叫 `backpack`、`student-bag`、`PPE-2026-Q1`、`SKU-88512004`——语义等价但命名完全不同。语义网通过 URI 解决了全局唯一标识，但没有内在机制处理跨系统的语义等价断言。

---

**二、新增第 9 章「哲学基础：EDD 的思想谱系与理论支撑」**

独立章节，专门介绍 EDD 方法论背后的四个哲学思想来源：

| 哲学人物 | 核心观点 | 对 EDD 的支撑 |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| **尼采** | 视角主义：不存在纯粹的客观知识，所有知识都是视角性的 | 多视图本体的哲学基础 |
| **海德格尔** | 在世存在：人类在"操心"中理解世界，闪现想法是自然涌现 | AI 主动提问机制的合理性 |
| **波普尔** | 三个世界：World 3（客观知识）独立于意识存在；World 2 与 World 3 有反馈回路 | 多视图分类框架（世界 1=运行产物，世界 2=闪现想法，世界 3=代码+RFC+架构） |
| **莱考夫 & 约翰逊** | 具身认知 + 概念隐喻：抽象概念通过隐喻从身体经验构建，隐喻是思维本身的结构 | 闪现式思维的认知科学依据；多视图的隐喻性 |

每节都包含：**人物与著作 → 核心观点 → 对 EDD 的支撑**，结构清晰，可独立阅读。<think>
The user hasn't sent a new message - the system just updated the open and recently viewed files. The last assistant response already completed writing the document. There's nothing new to respond to. I'll wait for the user's next input.
</think>
